summary: Dan Shapiro argues that asking contacts for generic introductions to investors is counterproductive and marks you as inexperienced. He outlines three reasons: not all investors are right for your company, it's lazy to offload the matchmaking work to your contact, and unprepared introductions will be lukewarm and ineffective. Instead, he prescribes a five-step process: research specific investors beforehand, pitch your contact first to build their enthusiasm, ask for specific named introductions, then ask for additional suggestions, and follow up with forwardable emails. The core message is that fundraising requires the same rigor and preparation as building the product itself.
key_points: Investors specialize by stage, sector, check size, and style — treating them as interchangeable shows inexperience
Asking for generic intros offloads research work onto your contact that only you are equipped to do well
A lukewarm introduction from someone who hasn't been sold on your company is worse than no introduction at all
The right approach is to research specific investors, pitch your contact first, then ask for named introductions with forwardable materials
Teams that invest the most effort in fundraising process tend to get the best results
money_quotes: There's no way to screw it up faster than going around asking, 'Hey, could you introduce me to some investors?'
Asking for introductions to 'investors' marks you as someone who doesn't really know what they're doing.
Somebody has to be the matchmaker. The right person to do that is you, since you know your company best.
Sound like a lot of work? It is. That's why you should do it, not your friend.
If the nicest thing they can say about your company is that it 'sounds interesting', the intro isn't going to go anywhere.
A great investor intro is about conveying enthusiasm. So you need to sell them, then give them simple tools to sell the investor.
Don't teach them; tease them. Show them just enough to get them to want more.
The teams that invest the most in fundraising seem to have the best results.
key_insight: Fundraising introductions are a sales process — you must do the research, pitch your contact first, and ask for specific named introductions rather than outsourcing the matchmaking to someone else.
